questions.question: A critical API endpoint that was working fine yesterday is now returning 500 errors. No code was deployed. Walk me through your debugging process.
questions.assesses: Systematic debugging, root cause analysis, production awareness

questions.question: You are given a feature request that is vaguely defined: 'Make the coaching messages smarter.' How do you go from this to a shipped feature?
questions.assesses: Ambiguity handling, requirements gathering, breaking down problems

questions.question: A database query that ran fine when there were 1,000 users is now taking 30 seconds with 100,000 users. How do you approach this?
questions.assesses: Performance debugging, database optimization thinking, scalability awareness

questions.question: You discover a security vulnerability in production at 11 PM on a Friday. What do you do?
questions.assesses: Incident response, prioritization under pressure, communication judgment

questions.question: You need to integrate with a third-party API that has poor documentation and inconsistent responses. How do you approach building a reliable integration?
questions.assesses: Defensive programming, resilience patterns, real-world pragmatism

questions.question: Two team members disagree on whether to use a new framework for a critical service. One wants stability, the other wants modern tooling. You are the tiebreaker. How do you decide?
questions.assesses: Technical decision-making, trade-off analysis, team dynamics

questions.question: You are asked to estimate how long a feature will take. You genuinely have no idea because the codebase is new to you. How do you give a useful estimate?
A background job that sends daily coaching nudges to 5,000 users is silently failing for 20% of users. Logs show no errors. How do you find the problem?
questions.assesses: Silent failure debugging, observability thinking, systematic investigation

questions.question: You wrote a feature, it works in staging, but fails in production. What are the most common reasons for this, and how do you systematically rule them out?
questions.assesses: Environment parity understanding, deployment awareness, systematic thinking

questions.question: You are reviewing a pull request from a senior developer. You think the approach is wrong. How do you handle it?
questions.assesses: Communication skills, code review maturity, professional courage

questions.question: How do you decide when to refactor existing code vs. work around it? Give me a real example from your experience.
questions.assesses: Technical debt judgment, pragmatism, cost-benefit analysis

questions.question: You need to design a system that processes incoming webhook events. Some events must be processed in order, some can be processed in parallel. How do you architect this?
questions.assesses: System design, concurrency thinking, ordering guarantees
